name: Taormina Sicilian Cuisine
tagline: Upscale Sicilian in the heart of Waikiki — handmade pasta, imported wines, and the best Italian food on this side of the Pacific.
mustOrder: any handmade pasta — the chef imports semolina from Sicily
branzino or fresh catch prepared Mediterranean-style
parking: Waikiki street parking on Lewers St; hotel valet options nearby
bestTime: Reservations recommended for dinner, especially Fri–Sat
talkStory: Taormina fills the fine Italian dining gap that Waikiki has always had. Named after the clifftop Sicilian town, the restaurant takes Southern Italian cuisine seriously — handmade pastas, seafood-forward dishes, and an Italian wine list that goes deep. The space is elegant without being stuffy, a welcome change from the resort restaurant formula. At 4.7 stars with nearly 3,000 reviews, it holds its own against Waikiki's best restaurants. Not cheap, but a legitimate splurge option for Italian food lovers.
